revision.text:
[[ja:八王子社 (南知多町)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hachioji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Shinojima Hachiojisya 2019-06 ac (1).jpg | location = 15 Doyama, Shinojima, Minamichita Town, Chita District, Aichi Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 40 | Latitude seconds = 33.5 | Latitude Direction = N | Longitude degrees = 137 | Longitude minutes = 00 | Longitude seconds = 17.0 | Longitude Direction = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Aichi | Deities = [[Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto]]
[[Ame-no-hohi|Ame-no-Hohino-Mikoto]]
[[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto]]
{{ill|Ikutsuhikone|en|Ikutsuhikone|qid=Q11147583|lt=Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|1=Ikutsuhikone}}
[[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto]]
[[Tagorihime|Takiribime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Ichikishima-hime|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Tagitsuhime|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto]] | Sacred Object = | type = | consecration_year = Shōō 1 (1288), September | Main Hall = | alternate_name = | Pilgrimage = | Main Festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Minamichita|qid=Q1203589|1=Minamichita}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.675972|N|137.004722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Hachioji Shrine''' (八王子社) is a [[shrine]] located at 15 Doyama, Shinojima, Minamichita Town, Chita District, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Of the two main shrines on Shinojima Island, Hachioji Shrine is referred to as the "male shrine," while [[Shimmei Shrine (Shinojima, Minamichita)|Shimmei Shrine]] is called the "female shrine." Hachioji Shrine is worshiped as a guardian of shipbuilding and maritime safety. == Deities == [[File:Shinojima Hachiojisya 2019-06 ac (2).jpg|thumb|Torii Gate]] * [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto]] * [[Ame-no-Hohi|Ame-no-Hohino-Mikoto]] * [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto]] * {{ill|Ikutsuhikone|en|Ikutsuhikone|qid=Q11147583|lt=Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto|1=Ikutsuhikone}} * [[Kumanokusubi|Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto]] * [[Tagorihime|Takiribime-no-Mikoto]] * [[Ichikishima-hime|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto]] * [[Tagitsuhime|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto]] == History == [[File:Shinojima Island Aerial photograph.jpg|thumb|180px|Shinojima Island, home to Hachioji Shrine]] According to legend recorded in the [[Nihon Shoki]], Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qid=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} visited Shinojima Island while traveling by sea from {{ill|Nakashima District|en|Nakashima District|qid=Q24875390|lt=Nakashima District|1=Nakashima District}} in {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|qid=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}} to {{ill|Atsumi District|en|Atsumi District|qid=Q482062|lt=Atsumi District|1=Atsumi District}} in {{ill|Mikawa Province|en|Mikawa Province|qid=Q768158|lt=Mikawa Province|1=Mikawa Province}}. She designated the island as a sacred territory and enshrined the deity's [[:en:ara-mitama|Ara-mitama]]. The shrine was established in September of Shōō 1 (1288) when deities were transferred from [[Miku Shrine]] in {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|qid=Q1131032|lt=Watarai District|1=Watarai District}}, {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|qid=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}} (currently in Ogicho, [[:en:Ise City|Ise City]]).[http://minamichita.net/jinjya/ History] Shinojima Shimmei Shrine In Eishō 14 (1517), a lion's head from Miku Shrine was dedicated to Hachioji Shrine, where it has since been preserved as a sacred treasure.[http://shinojimadentou.jp/ Shinojima's Two Shrines] Shinojima Traditional Skills Preservation == Shikinen Sengu == During the [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] of [[Ise Grand Shrine]], held every 20 years, the old materials from Ise are donated to Shimmei Shrine on Shinojima to rebuild its halls. Subsequently, Shimmei Shrine's old materials are used to reconstruct the halls of Hachioji Shrine. The old materials from Hachioji Shrine are repurposed into numerous small shrines scattered across the island.[http://shinojimadentou.jp/onsei/ Shimmei Shrine & Hachioji Shrine] Shinojima Traditional Skills Preservation Shinojima is the only place in Japan where the entire set of materials from Ise Shrine is relocated and repurposed. == Festivals == === New Year's Festival and Daimyo Procession === Every January 3 and 4, New Year's ceremonies, including a grand daimyo procession, take place at Hachioji Shrine and [[Shimmei Shrine (Shinojima, Minamichita)|Shimmei Shrine]]. The festival symbolizes the journey of the male deity worshiped at Hachioji Shrine, known as "Ojinjikisama," to visit the female deity at Shimmei Shrine, where he spends the night before returning.[http://www.shinojima-matsuri.jp/january.html New Year's Procession] Shinojima Festivals On January 3, men playing the roles of unluckiest villagers perform dances in a procession accompanied by chants. At 6 p.m., the journey of "Ojinjikisama" begins. To preserve the sacredness of the event, viewing the procession is forbidden, and the island's electricity is temporarily shut off to ensure privacy. The priest carries Ojijnikisama from Hachioji Shrine to Shimmei Shrine, where drumming marks his arrival. Islanders then rush to pray at Shimmei Shrine. On January 4, the festival continues with additional dances, and the male deity is returned to Hachioji Shrine by the afternoon. The festival concludes as the male deity returns to his shrine, bringing blessings for the year ahead. == location == * 470-3505 15 Doyama, Shinojima, Minamichita Town, Chita District, Aichi Prefecture * Access: A short walk from the Shinojima Ferry Terminal == References == {{reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://minamichita.net/jinjya/ Hachioji Shrine] Shinojima Shimmei Shrine * [http://www.shinojima-aichi.com/2014/05/21/hachioji/ Hachioji Shrine] Shinojima {{shinto-stub}} < {{translated page|ja|八王子社 (南知多町)|version=80524966|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:八王子社 (南知多町)]] {{wikidata link|Q97181502}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Minamichita, Aichi]] [[Category:History of Minamichita]] [[Category:Mishima in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
